scope: Release APK native artifact download performance on a real Android Emulator using ADB UIAutomator and a request-counting gateway proxy.

mobile_app.head: 6a1cfa1
mobile_app.commit: test: add release file download avd smoke

source.head: 7e436f7e
source.commit: fix: order mobile native transcript pages by record time

release_apk.build_warning: file_picker and mobile_scanner still use plugin-applied Kotlin Gradle Plugin; Flutter reports this as a future deprecation warning.

ui_smoke.automation: ADB UIAutomator XML plus adb input tap; no Flutter Driver.

acceptance_notes: The run started two disposable real /home/bfly/yunwei/test_ccb2 projects and a loopback-only server-wide gateway.
The release APK was seeded with the paired profile, installed, and launched without Flutter Driver.
ADB UIAutomator opened test_ccb2_alpha/mobile_probe from the real server-wide project list, tapped the requested agent chip, and explicitly refreshed the selected-agent conversation.
The app rendered the 8.0 MB downloadable artifact chip from a provider-native transcript fixture and showed the Saved snackbar after tap.
The gateway proxy observed exactly one file download request, returned 8,388,608 bytes, and the response SHA256 matched the seeded artifact.
Device metrics after a 5-second post-save settle showed no app wake locks, no FATAL/ANR/OOM logcat marker, and no skipped-frame logcat marker.
residual_risks: This closes release 8 MiB native artifact download performance for the real server-wide Android Emulator path.
